# Professional Profile

> This file is your professional identity. It's read by most modules to understand who you are beyond your CV — your strengths, working style, what energizes you, and the story you tell about your career. Fill it in honestly. The quality of every output depends on the quality of what's here.

---

## Professional Summary

<!-- 3–5 sentences. Who are you as a PM? What's your area of focus or expertise? What kind of products, problems, or contexts have you worked in? Write this as you'd introduce yourself in a professional setting — not a job application opener, but the honest version. -->

[Your professional summary here]

## Key Skills and Domains

<!-- List your actual areas of expertise. Be specific — not "product management" but what kind: B2B SaaS, consumer, marketplace, platform, data products, etc. Include relevant domain knowledge (e.g., fintech, logistics, developer tools), and any technical skills that are genuinely yours. Don't list things you'd struggle to talk about in depth. -->

[Your skills and domains here]

## Work Style and Preferences

<!-- How do you actually work? Are you strong on strategy or execution — or both, and when? Do you prefer small teams or large orgs? Autonomous work or collaborative processes? High-ambiguity environments or well-defined problems? Be honest — this helps the system flag roles that might be a bad fit, not just good ones. -->

[Your work style and preferences here]

[Your strengths here]

The more specific you are here, the more useful the goal-setting and pipeline modules will be. -->

**Want to avoid:**
